terms.definition: Scatter (or 3D scatter) of low-dimensional coordinates representing high-dimensional samples; often colored by label or metadata.
Plotting learned or reduced representations in 2D/3D to inspect geometry; common for neural network embeddings and manifold methods.
Mapping a categorical or continuous metadata column to point color (and legend or colorbar) on an embedding scatter.
Loss of detail when many points overlap in a scatter; mitigated by alpha transparency, subsampling, hexbin, or density contours.
A dense region of points in an embedding plot; suggests shared local geometry but requires validation before treating as a formal cluster.
Inspecting misclassified or low-confidence points on an embedding—often colored by true label and marked by prediction error—to complement confusion matrices.
